summary:
Defective Rb gene phosphorylation impairs normal cell cycle control; consequently, unregulated cellular proliferation ensues. The inability to correctly stop at crucial stages permits excessive growth potentially resulting from undetected errors during DNA synthesis and mitosis phases. \boxed{Uncontrolled Cell Division}

The summary captures that when there’s an issue with how the Rb gene gets modified by phosphate groups, its job as a checkpoint guardian fails—leading directly into unrestricted cell multiplication because these safeguards are no longer effective against premature entry into subsequent rounds of reproduction cycles within tissues where they reside abundantly like retina tissue among others prone for retinal tumors formation given their inherent vulnerability towards such dysregulations specifically linked back here too!
